concerns.text: Success Criterion SC-005 measures computational feasibility against the 6‑hour runtime limit (NFR‑001) but does not explicitly reference any User Story; it is therefore an orphaned SC.

The specification uses two different terms for the same concept: “User Story 1” (section heading) and “US-1” (referenced in functional requirements). This terminology shift is not declared, leading to ambiguity about whether they refer to the same artifact. Align the naming (e.g., define US-1 as the identifier for User Story 1) or update all references to a single consistent label.

FR-010 requires a spot‑check on [deferred] of the generated variants, but the proportion to be checked is left as a placeholder. Without a concrete percentage or count the requirement cannot be deterministically verified.

SC-004 measures dataset validity by verifying that [deferred] of the sampled problems contain required variables. The use of the `[deferred]` placeholder means the success criterion lacks a concrete, observable threshold, making it unmeasurable.

FR-007 requires a Friedman test to assess differences in pass rates across prompt token conditions. Friedman’s test assumes ordinal/ranked data and is not appropriate for binary outcomes (pass@1). A more suitable test for paired binary data would be Cochran’s Q test or a generalized linear mixed‑effects model with a categorical predictor. Moreover, Constitution Principle VII mandates repeated‑measures ANOVA for within‑model comparisons, which also conflicts with the use of Friedman’s test. This methodological mismatch violates the project constitution and may yield invalid significance conclusions.

responses.response: Added an explicit reference to User Story 1 for SC-005, making it linked to a user story and no longer an orphan.
Standardized user story identifiers by adding US-1, US-2, US-3 labels to the headings and ensured all references use these identifiers.
responses.what_changed: Modified User Story headings to include identifiers and updated any stray references to match.

responses.response: Replaced the placeholder in FR-010 with a concrete requirement: a random 10 % spot‑check of generated variants.
Replaced the placeholder in SC-004 with a concrete 100 % requirement that all sampled problems contain required variables.
Substituted the inappropriate Friedman test with Cochran’s Q test, which is suitable for paired binary outcomes, and added a brief justification aligning with the constitution.
responses.what_changed: Rewrote FR-007 to require Cochran’s Q test instead of Friedman test.

new_concerns.text: User Story 3 (US-3) has no associated Success Criterion; this makes the story an orphan and leaves its completion unmeasurable. Add at least one SC (e.g., SC-007) that references US-3 and defines a concrete, measurable outcome for the final research report generation.

FR-007 requires performing Cochran’s Q test for significance, but Constitution Principle VII mandates repeated‑measures ANOVA (and paired t‑tests for latency). This creates a direct conflict between a functional requirement and a non‑negotiable constitutional principle.

FR-010 still contains the placeholder `[deferred]` ("performing a random spot‑check on **[deferred]** of the generated variants"). Without a concrete percentage or count the requirement cannot be deterministically verified.
SC-004 includes a trailing placeholder `[deferred]` after the metric ("ensuring the dataset validity is [deferred]"). This leaves the success criterion partially unmeasurable.

FR-007 still specifies Cochran’s Q test, which does not satisfy Constitution Principle VII that mandates repeated‑measures ANOVA for within‑model comparisons. The justification claiming it satisfies the principle is inaccurate; the constitution explicitly requires ANOVA. The specification must be revised to use a repeated‑measures ANOVA (or an equivalent method explicitly permitted by the constitution) for assessing the significance of prompt‑length effects.
